articles.title: After the Minnesota surge, ICE is moving to a quieter enforcement approach
articles.summary: ICE seems to be changing from aggressive immigration enforcement on city streets to an apparent return to operations that rely heavily on local law enforcement. But even in Florida, where sheriffs are

articles.title: Trump Pledged a Quick End to the Iran War, but He Hasn’t Explained How
articles.summary: President Trump bet that American firepower could cow Iran into compliance. So far, Iran’s leaders have been unwilling to quit.

articles.title: Judge halts Trump effort requiring colleges to show they don't consider race in admissions
articles.summary: A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.

articles.title: Missing Airman Raises Concerns That Iran Could Gain Leverage Over the U.S.
articles.summary: Since 1979, Iran has repeatedly used Americans and Europeans detained on its territory to win concessions over more powerful adversaries.

articles.title: China's Communist Party investigates ex-Xinjiang leader Ma Xingrui
articles.summary: Ma Xingrui is a member of the party's Central Committee and served as party secretary of the Xinjiang region in China's northwest from 2021-2025.

articles.title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
articles.summary: An F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran and an A-10 Warthog crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, officials said on Friday. Two airmen were rescued, and one is unaccounted for.

articles.title: Trump Contemplates Other Cabinet Changes as He Faces Political Clock
articles.summary: With the midterms approaching, the president may be running out of time to get new cabinet members confirmed without bipartisan support.

articles.title: 'London Falling': A teenage imposter, an aging gangster and a body in the Thames

articles.title: Opinion: Humanity's hopes ascended with Artemis II
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the successful launch of NASA's Artemis II this week. The four astronauts aboard will travel around the moon.

articles.title: Trump Wants to Make Deportation Deals. Autocrats Are Ready to Listen.
articles.summary: The White House has turned deportations, a signature domestic issue, into a major piece of foreign policy. Here’s what we know about the program.

articles.title: Iran war enters its 6th week as military searches for downed jet crew member
articles.summary: The war in Iran enters its 6th week as the search continues for the missing U.S. service member who bailed out of a fighter jet shot down over Iran on Friday.

articles.title: The busiest place you've never seen
articles.summary: Photographer Julia Gunther and writer-filmmaker Nick Schönfeld chronicle the rhythms of daily life on Tristan da Cunha, the world's most remote inhabited island.

articles.title: Buttercream wool and jelly bean eyes: The art of the Easter lamb cake
articles.summary: The cakes – usually baked in the shape of a lamb using a special pan – have a long history in Central Europe, from the German osterlamm, to the Polish baranek wielkanocny, to the Alsatian lammele.

articles.title: When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems
articles.summary: As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.

articles.title: New Attorney General, Same Albatross: Trump’s Quest for Retribution
articles.summary: The name atop the Justice Department’s organizational chart matters less than the presence of a president whose demands for revenge have become so extreme that even his most obsequious appointees have

articles.title: ‘I Got Back Every Penny’: Inside Trump’s Supercharged Tax Season
articles.summary: The law Republicans passed last year has so far been largely imperceptible to most Americans. That’s changing as tens of millions file their taxes this spring.

articles.title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
articles.summary: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.

articles.title: Trump's criticism of NATO raises questions of whether — or how — the U.S. could leave
articles.summary: President Trump has complained that some NATO states aren't letting him use bases in the war against Iran. His criticism has left many wondering if the U.S. may try to withdraw from the alliance.

articles.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to get internet
articles.summary: As the Israel and the U.S. continue striking Iran, Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey to find an Internet connection with access cut off in Iran

articles.title: Seville, Spain's Holy Week blends faith, tradition and spectacle
articles.summary: Even as religious belief declines in Spain, the processions at Seville's Semana Santa — the Holy Week lead-up to Easter — draw crowds moved by music, tradition and powerful emotion.

articles.title: A U.S. jet goes down over Iran, a U.S. official confirms
articles.summary: A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
